---
title: "Estimation (FOCE/FOCEI)"
---

ferx-core supports five estimation methods: FOCE, FOCEI, SAEM, Gauss-Newton,
and a GN+FOCEI hybrid. FOCEI is the default. All methods share the same
two-loop structure: an outer loop over population parameters and an inner loop
over per-subject empirical Bayes estimates (EBEs).

## FOCE vs FOCEI

**FOCE** (First-Order Conditional Estimation) linearises the model around the
EBE η̂ and evaluates the residual variance R at the population mean (η = 0):

$$\text{OFV}_i = (y - f_0)^T \tilde{R}^{-1} (y - f_0) + \log|\tilde{R}|$$

where $f_0 = f(\hat{\eta}) - H\hat{\eta}$ and $\tilde{R} = H\Omega H^T + R(f_0)$.

**FOCEI** adds the *interaction* term — the residual variance $V(\hat{\eta})$
is evaluated at η̂ rather than η = 0, capturing the dependence of residual
error on individual predictions:

$$\text{OFV}_i = (y - \hat{f})^T V^{-1} (y - \hat{f}) + \hat{\eta}^T \Omega^{-1} \hat{\eta} + \log|\tilde{R}|$$

FOCEI is more accurate when the error model is proportional or combined.
Use FOCE only for additive-error models where the interaction term is zero.

## The two-loop structure

### Outer loop — population parameters

Minimises the population OFV = Σᵢ OFVᵢ over θ, Ω, σ. Parameters are
internally log-transformed (theta, sigma) and Cholesky-factored (omega) so the
optimizer works on an unconstrained packed vector:

```
x = [log(θ_free), chol(Ω_BSV), log(σ), chol(Ω_IOV)]
```

Available optimizers (set via `optimizer` in `[fit_options]`):

| Key | Algorithm | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `slsqp` | NLopt SLSQP | **Default.** Gradient-based, handles bounds well |
| `bobyqa` | NLopt BOBYQA | Derivative-free; robust when FD gradients are noisy |
| `nlopt_lbfgs` | NLopt L-BFGS | Good for large parameter spaces |
| `mma` | NLopt MMA | Method of Moving Asymptotes |
| `bfgs` | Built-in BFGS | Quasi-Newton with backtracking line search |
| `lbfgs` | Built-in L-BFGS | Memory-efficient BFGS |
| `trust_region` | Newton trust-region (argmin) | AD gradient + BHHH Hessian; Steihaug CG subproblem |

**Global search:** setting `global_search = true` runs NLopt CRS2-LM
(Controlled Random Search) as a gradient-free pre-search before the local
optimizer. Helps escape local minima on poorly-identified models. Budget is
controlled by `global_maxeval` (default: `200 * (n_params + 1)`).

### Inner loop — empirical Bayes estimates

Minimises the individual NLL per subject independently (parallelised via rayon):

$$\text{NLL}(\eta) = \frac{1}{2}\left[\eta^T\Omega^{-1}\eta + \log|\Omega| + \sum_j\left(\frac{(y_j - f_j(\hat{\eta}))^2}{V_j(\hat{\eta})} + \log V_j(\hat{\eta})\right)\right]$$

Uses L-BFGS with automatic differentiation gradients (preferred) or finite
differences. Falls back to Nelder-Mead simplex if L-BFGS fails to converge.

**Warm starting:** EBEs from the previous outer iteration seed the inner
optimizer, drastically reducing inner iterations in later stages when
parameters change slowly.

**EBE convergence guard:** if more than `max_unconverged_frac` (default 0.1)
of subjects have unconverged EBEs, the outer optimizer receives OFV = ∞ for
that step, preventing convergence to a solution with unreliable EBEs.

## Convergence criteria

| Loop | Criterion | Default |
|---|---|---|
| Outer | gradient norm < `outer_gtol` | `1e-6` |
| Outer | max iterations | `maxiter = 500` |
| Inner | gradient norm < `inner_tol` | `1e-4` |
| Inner | max iterations | `inner_maxiter = 200` |

`inner_tol = 1e-4` matches the precision of NONMEM's default inner-loop
convergence (SIGDIGITS ≈ 3). Tighter values over-converge EBEs relative to the
Sheiner-Beal linearisation error and can slow FOCEI fits 10–15× without
changing the final OFV.

## OFV / AIC / BIC

$$\text{OFV} = -2\log L \qquad \text{AIC} = \text{OFV} + 2p \qquad \text{BIC} = \text{OFV} + p\log N$$

where $p$ is the number of estimated parameters and $N$ is the number of
observations.

## Covariance step

When `covariance = true`, ferx computes the observed Fisher information matrix
via finite-difference Hessian of the population OFV at convergence:

- **Standard errors** = $\sqrt{\text{diag}(\hat{\Sigma})}$
- **%RSE** = $|\text{SE} / \hat{\theta}| \times 100$
- **Omega SEs** derived via delta method from the Cholesky parameterisation

If the Hessian is not positive definite or cannot be inverted, ferx emits a
warning (`"Covariance step failed"`) and sets `covariance_status = Failed`; SE
fields are `None`. A future release will include eigenvalue diagnostics in the
warning to help distinguish near-zero eigenvalues from identifiability problems
([ferx-core#17](https://github.com/FeRx-NLME/ferx-core/issues/17)).

## SAEM

Stochastic Approximation EM replaces the deterministic inner loop with
Metropolis-Hastings sampling, following the Monolix two-phase schedule:

- **Phase 1 (exploration, γ = 1):** rapid basin finding; default 150 iterations
- **Phase 2 (convergence, γ = 1/k):** decreasing-weight average; default 250 iterations

Each SAEM iteration:

1. **E-step:** sample η per subject via MH random walk
   ($\eta_\text{prop} = \eta + \delta_i \cdot L \cdot z$, $z \sim N(0,I)$)
2. **SA update:** $S_2 \leftarrow (1-\gamma_k)S_2 + \gamma_k \cdot \frac{1}{N}\sum_i \eta_i\eta_i^T$
3. **M-step for Ω:** $\Omega_k = S_2$ (closed form, with structural zeros projected out)
4. **M-step for θ and σ:** NLopt minimises the conditional observation NLL with
   ETAs fixed. When `mu_referencing = true` (default), log-normal thetas use the
   closed-form EM update instead: $\log\theta_j \leftarrow \log\theta_j + \gamma_k\overline{\eta_j}$
5. **Adaptation:** per-subject MH step sizes $\delta_i$ adjusted every
   `adapt_interval` iterations to target ~40% acceptance rate

Post-SAEM: standard FOCE inner loop refines EBEs from the SAEM ETAs, then
FOCE OFV, AIC, BIC, and (optionally) the covariance matrix are computed so
results are directly comparable with FOCE fits.

```
[fit_options]
  method        = saem
  n_exploration = 150
  n_convergence = 250
  n_mh_steps    = 3
  seed          = 12345
  covariance    = true
```

SAEM + FOCEI polish (recommended for complex models):

```
[fit_options]
  method     = [saem, focei]
  covariance = true
```

**Limitation:** SAEM is not supported with IOV models.

## Gauss-Newton

The Gauss-Newton optimizer exploits the nonlinear-least-squares structure of
the FOCE objective using the BHHH Hessian approximation:

$$H_\text{BHHH} = 4 \sum_i g_i g_i^T$$

where $g_i = \nabla_x \text{NLL}_i$ is the per-subject gradient. Levenberg-Marquardt
damping $H_\text{LM} = H_\text{BHHH} + \lambda\,\text{diag}(H_\text{BHHH})$ stabilises
steps away from the minimum; $\lambda$ adapts: ×0.3 on success, ×10 on rejection.

| Method | Key | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Pure GN | `gn` | Typically converges in 10–30 iterations |
| GN + FOCEI hybrid | `gn_hybrid` | GN finds the basin; FOCEI polishes (up to 100 iter via SLSQP) |

Use `gn` for fast feedback during model development; `gn_hybrid` when you want
robustness as well as speed.

## Inter-Occasion Variability (IOV)

IOV models occasion-to-occasion shifts in PK parameters within a subject.
Kappa (κ) is the IOV random effect, re-drawn independently per occasion:

$$\kappa_{ik} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Omega_\text{IOV})$$

**Option A — diagonal IOV** (independent kappas):

```
[parameters]
  kappa KAPPA_CL ~ 0.05
  kappa KAPPA_V  ~ 0.03

[individual_parameters]
  CL = TVCL * exp(ETA_CL + KAPPA_CL)

[fit_options]
  iov_column = OCC
```

**Option B — block IOV** (correlated kappas):

```
block_kappa (KAPPA_CL, KAPPA_V) = [0.05, 0.01, 0.03]
```

Mixed Option A + B declarations are supported; a name may not appear in both.

The inner loop uses `find_ebe_iov` instead of `find_ebe` when kappas are
present, jointly optimising BSV etas and all per-occasion kappas. The outer
loop packs IOV omega after sigma in the packed vector. **AD is not used for IOV
gradients** — finite differences are used instead.

## Sampling Importance Resampling (SIR)

SIR is an optional post-estimation step that provides non-parametric parameter
uncertainty estimates, more robust than the asymptotic covariance matrix for
small datasets or boundary estimates.

1. Draw M parameter vectors from a multivariate Student-t proposal centred on
   $\hat{\theta}$ with covariance $\hat{\Sigma}$ and degrees of freedom ν
   (default ν = 5). The heavy tails relative to Normal help cover the posterior
   when the proposal covariance underestimates uncertainty.
2. Compute importance weights from OFV differences
3. Resample m vectors proportional to weights

The effective sample size (ESS) diagnostic indicates fit quality: ESS > m is
excellent; ESS < 100 suggests the proposal is a poor match.

```
[fit_options]
  method           = focei
  covariance       = true
  sir              = true
  sir_samples      = 1000
  sir_resamples    = 250
  sir_seed         = 42
  sir_df           = 5       # Student-t degrees of freedom (default 5)
  sir_keep_samples = false   # set true to access raw proposal draws
```

Reference: Dosne et al. (2017), *J Pharmacokinet Pharmacodyn* 44(6):539-562.

## Mu-referencing

When `mu_referencing = true` (default), ferx detects log-normal parameters
from `[individual_parameters]` and re-centres ETA estimates on the current
population mean each inner iteration. This improves conditioning for the SAEM
M-step (closed-form EM update) and speeds inner-loop convergence for
gradient-based methods. Disable with `mu_referencing = false` to reproduce
pre-automatic-mu behaviour.

## Key fit_options reference

| Key | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `method` | `focei` | `foce`, `focei`, `saem`, `gn`, `gn_hybrid`, or a list e.g. `[saem, focei]` |
| `maxiter` | `500` | Max outer iterations |
| `covariance` | `true` | Compute covariance matrix and SEs |
| `optimizer` | `slsqp` | See optimizer table above |
| `inner_maxiter` | `200` | Max inner (EBE) iterations per subject |
| `inner_tol` | `1e-4` | Inner convergence gradient-norm tolerance |
| `scale_params` | `true` | Scale packed parameters to O(1) before optimizer |
| `global_search` | `false` | Run CRS2-LM pre-search before local optimizer |
| `bloq_method` | `drop` | `drop` or `m3` (Beal M3 likelihood for BLOQ rows) |
| `iov_column` | — | Name of occasion column (required for IOV models) |
| `optimizer_trace` | `false` | Write per-iteration CSV to `/tmp/ferx_trace_<pid>.csv` |

## Relevant source files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `estimation/outer_optimizer.rs` | `optimize_population()`, NLopt/BFGS/trust-region dispatch |
| `estimation/inner_optimizer.rs` | `find_ebe()`, gradient resolution (AD vs FD), EBE warm-start |
| `estimation/saem.rs` | SAEM algorithm, MH sampling, two-phase schedule |
| `estimation/gauss_newton.rs` | Gauss-Newton / BHHH with LM damping and line search |
| `estimation/trust_region.rs` | Newton trust-region with Steihaug CG subproblem |
| `estimation/parameterization.rs` | Pack/unpack, mu-referencing, log/Cholesky transforms |
| `estimation/sir.rs` | SIR importance resampling |
| `stats/likelihood.rs` | `foce_subject_nll()`, `foce_population_nll()`, CWRES |
| `stats/residual_error.rs` | Residual variance by error model, IWRES |
